message:
I'll admit it—I was *that person* who kept saying "yeah, yeah, I'll update the smoke alarms" for like... two years. My partner didn't even bother rolling their eyes anymore.

Then one random Tuesday, I burned dinner (garlic bread, don't ask), and our ancient alarm went off like a banshee. While I was frantically waving a dish towel around like a maniac, my partner just stood there with the most satisfied smile I've ever seen. They didn't say a word. Didn't have to.

But here's the thing—that moment actually made me realize how much I *rely* on that little device. It caught something that could've been worse. And suddenly those "boring" safety habits didn't feel boring anymore.

We've since done a proper walk-through: tested everything, mapped out our escape route, the whole deal. Turns out when you actually *see* how these things work, it's kind of satisfying.

Have you gotten your alarms checked recently, or are you still in the "I know I should" phase like I was? 😄
